core_mechanism: spectral zeta-function regularization: assigning a finite value to a divergent sum of zero-point contributions over the discrete eigenmode spectrum of a differential operator (set by boundary/compactification conditions) via analytic continuation of the associated spectral zeta function
brief_reason: The abstract states the vacuum (Casimir) energy and pressure of a field with discretized modes (fixed by bag boundary conditions, a magnetic field, and a compactified dimension) are computed 'using the zeta function technique', i.e. by regularizing the divergent zero-point mode sum through its spectral zeta function.
